title: Proteomic subtyping benchmark on open PRIDE/CPTAC data
context: This is a backlog task for after the harness is stable (after M3). Proteomic subtyping benchmarks using open PRIDE or CPTAC proteogenomics data extend the project beyond transcriptomics/expression. PRIDE datasets are typically CC-BY or CC0 per accession; CPTAC open tier is usable. Each PRIDE accession must have its licence verified individually through the gate-002 checklist. An independent external cohort is required. This task follows the same gate + definition-object + audit + baseline pattern as the M1/M2 benchmarks but with proteomic feature modality.
objective: Define and deliver a proteomic subtyping benchmark on open PRIDE or CPTAC open-tier data: triage candidate datasets through the gate-002 checklist (per-accession CC-BY/CC0 licence verified for PRIDE; CPTAC open tier confirmed), create the benchmark definition object with proteomic featureSpec, patient-grouped splits, and a declared independent external cohort; run the full leakage-audit suite; document the datasheet.
acceptanceCriteria: Per-accession CC-BY/CC0 licence is verified and recorded for each PRIDE dataset used; CPTAC open-tier status is confirmed; each source passes the gate-002 checklist with a committed gate artifact

A benchmark definition object is created for proteomic subtyping with featureSpec specifying the proteomic modality (e.g. mass-spectrometry quantified protein intensities), labelSpec, patient-grouped splits, and a declared independent external cohort from a different study

The full leakage-audit suite passes in CI for the proteomic benchmark; the batch/centre confound check is especially important given platform differences across proteomic datasets and must be run and its result committed

A datasheet is published covering the proteomic data composition, collection, processing (sample preparation, instrument type, quantification method), de-identification, licence, and batch/centre/platform structure
An independent external cohort from a different PRIDE or CPTAC study is declared and its independence is confirmed by the domain reviewer
Domain reviewer and licence+de-id reviewer sign-offs are recorded
output: A committed proteomic subtyping benchmark definition object with per-accession licence gate records, patient-grouped splits with external cohort, full leakage-audit passage in CI, and a datasheet — extending the benchmark portfolio to proteomic modalities.
